question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that validate() does not verify that signatures in the collection correspond to distinct nodes, allowing an attacker to submit N copies of the same node's VERIFIED signature to artificially inflate the stake sum for a fraudulent fileHashAsHex group?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio()) check, where the equals() comparison may fail for a BigDecimal with value 0 but scale != 0 (e.g., 0.0 vs 0), causing the zero-ratio bypass to be skipped and consensus validation to proceed with a zero threshold, accepting any signature?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a very large collection of VERIFIED signatures with distinct fileHashAsHex values, causing the HashMultimap to consume excessive heap memory, triggering an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the importer process and halts all stream ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the debugStake variable tracks only the maximum stake seen across all hash groups, and the error message 'Consensus not reached for file %s with %d/%d stake' leaks the actual stake distribution, allowing an attacker to calibrate subsequent attacks to stay just below the consensus threshold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the ordering of the signatures collection—since filename and totalStake are taken from the first element—by ensuring the first element in the collection is a crafted signature with a manipulated node, while legitimate signatures follow, causing the threshold to be computed from attacker-controlled data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting signatures where getFileHashAsHex() returns an extremely long string (e.g., megabytes), causing the HashMultimap key to consume excessive memory and CPU during hashing, leading to a denial-of-service that stalls the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that setStatus(StreamFileSignature.SignatureStatus.CONSENSUS_REACHED) is called on signature objects inside validate(), and if those objects are shared references used elsewhere in the system, the status mutation could corrupt the state of other components that rely on the original VERIFIED status?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting a race condition where validate() is called concurrently on the same Collection<StreamFileSignature> from multiple threads, causing the signatureHashMap to be populated inconsistently and the consensusCount to be computed incorrectly, leading to either false consensus or false rejection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a signature collection where the first signature's node returns a negative totalStake value, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a negative threshold, making canReachConsensus() return true for any stake value including 0, and thus accepting fraudulent consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the BigDecimal precision in getStakeRequiredForConsensus()—specifically the multiply(consensusRatio) with a 19-decimal-place ratio—to produce floating-point rounding artifacts that make the threshold slightly higher than intended, causing borderline-legitimate consensus attempts to fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that validate() does not check whether all signatures in the collection refer to the same filename, allowing an attacker to mix signatures from different stream files in a single call, causing the consensus check to be performed against a mismatched set of signatures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the consensusRatio is read from commonDownloaderProperties at validation time (not cached), and if an attacker can trigger a configuration reload that sets consensusRatio to 1.0, all existing legitimate signature sets will fail consensus and the importer will stall?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by injecting a collection where all VERIFIED signatures have the same fileHashAsHex but their nodes' getStake() values sum to exactly one unit below the stakeRequiredForConsensus threshold (exploiting CEILING rounding), causing canReachConsensus() to return false and blocking ingestion indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that HashMultimap allows multiple values per key, and if the same StreamFileSignature object is added to the collection multiple times, it will appear multiple times in the multimap value set, causing stake to be double-counted and potentially falsely satisfying canReachConsensus()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not validate the integrity of the ConsensusNode objects attached to signatures, allowing an attacker to attach a ConsensusNode with a getStake() that differs from the node's actual registered stake, inflating or deflating the stake sum?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce a minimum number of signatures, so a single VERIFIED signature from a node holding exactly 1/3 of total stake can satisfy consensus, and if an attacker can forge or replay that one signature, they can make the importer accept a fraudulent stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not check for duplicate nodeIds across signatures in the same hash group, allowing an attacker to submit multiple signatures from the same node (each with the node's full stake) to artificially inflate the stake sum for a fraudulent hash group?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method processes all hash groups sequentially and sets CONSENSUS_REACHED on the first group that meets the threshold, and if an attacker can inject a fraudulent hash group that is processed before the legitimate one, the importer will accept the fraudulent stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not verify that the totalStake reported by different nodes in the same collection is consistent, allowing an attacker to inject a signature from a node reporting a different totalStake than the first node, which is silently ignored, potentially masking a stake manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that the signatures collection is non-null, causing a NullPointerException when null is passed, crashing the validation thread and halting stream ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce a maximum collection size, allowing an attacker to inject an arbitrarily large collection of VERIFIED signatures, causing the HashMultimap to grow unboundedly and exhaust heap memory, crashing the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that signatures in the collection correspond to the same stream file type, allowing an attacker to mix record stream and event stream signatures, causing the consensus check to be performed against a mismatched set?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that the fileHash bytes are of the expected length (256 bytes as seen in tests), allowing an attacker to inject signatures with truncated or extended fileHash values that produce the same hex string as a legitimate hash, falsely merging hash groups?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that the SignatureType of all signatures in the collection is consistent, allowing an attacker to inject signatures with a weak or broken SignatureType that passes NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() but represents a different security level?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the validate() method does not enforce that the StreamType of all signatures in the collection is consistent, allowing an attacker to inject signatures from a different stream type that happen to have VERIFIED status, polluting the hash group and affecting the stake calculation?
